title: The Woodcarver's Legacy
content: Taro is a third-generation woodcarver whose family workshop has stood at the edge of Springtown's forest for over seventy years. His grandfather built the workshop in 1948, and each generation has added their name to the main beam. The workshop is known for its intricate carvings that adorn Springtown - from Hana's tea shop sign to Sakura's picture frames and Mio's festival decorations. Taro is known for his practice of hiding small carved blossoms in every frame he makes for Sakura. He works primarily with local cedar and oak, and can read a tree's history through its growth rings. Despite his rough hands and quiet manner, Taro's work is extraordinarily delicate. He has not yet found an apprentice but keeps the workshop open to visitors who want to learn.
